steps.title: Connect the question to the discriminant
steps.content:
The equation is quadratic, and the question asks when it has more than one real solution. A quadratic has two real solutions exactly when its graph crosses the x-axis twice; algebraically, that means the discriminant is positive.

In $49x^2 + bx + 36 = 0$, $a = 49$ and $c = 36$, so:
$$b^2 - 4(49)(36) \gt 0$$
$$b^2 - 7056 \gt 0$$
$$b^2 \gt 7056$$

steps.title: Test which choice satisfies the condition
steps.content:
We need $b^2 \gt 7056$, which means $|b| \gt 84$.
  • Choice A — '$-93$': $b = -93$ gives $(-93)^2 = 8649 \gt 7056$ ✓
  • Choice B — '$-84$': $b = -84$ gives $(-84)^2 = 7056 ot\gt 7056$ ✗
  • Choice C — '$6$': $b = 6$ gives $(6)^2 = 36 ot\gt 7056$ ✗
  • Choice D — '$42$': $b = 42$ gives $(42)^2 = 1764 ot\gt 7056$ ✗
Only the credited option satisfies the condition. That confirms A is correct.
